problem_title: Travel Rule workflows slow and fragmented
problem_description: Compliance teams managing MAS Travel Rule obligations struggle with disconnected message routing and incomplete beneficiary data. This fragmentation slows case reviews and weakens audit readiness across multi-jurisdictional regulatory demands.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates Travel Rule messages, routing status, exception handling and beneficiary data into one workflow with linked cases and audit evidence to streamline compliance under MAS requirements.
workflow_title: From Travel Rule message to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY routes MAS-specific Travel Rule messages through status tracking, exception handling, case creation, evidence preservation and compliance reporting workflows for VASPs.

workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit

faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for MAS Travel Rule compliance?
faq_1_answer: SFTY supports MAS Travel Rule readiness by connecting provider outputs into structured workflows, cases, decisions and audit-ready evidence tailored for VASPs managing jurisdiction-specific compliance.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle Travel Rule workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes Travel Rule messages, tracks status and exceptions, applies MAS-specific policies, and creates cases for review, integrating these into broader compliance workflows for structured operational control.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve Travel Rule evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves complete audit trails of Travel Rule decisions, message histories, exception handling, and evidence packages to support regulatory reviews and inspection readiness under MAS requirements.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY connects existing Travel Rule, KYC, KYT and screening providers into one compliance layer without disrupting current systems, enabling coordinated workflows and consolidated evidence management.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace current Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers; it complements them by structuring their outputs into explainable workflows, cases, evidence and reports aligned with MAS Travel Rule obligations.
final_title: Structure MAS Travel Rule compliance workflows
